steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence ends a complete independent clause and then introduces a list of exoplanets separated by semicolons. The blank needs punctuation that correctly links the clause to the list.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — comma before the list: A comma cannot introduce a list that uses semicolons as separators; it also wrongly attaches the distance phrase to 'Earth.' ✗
  • Choice B — colon after the complete clause: A colon correctly introduces a list after an independent clause. The first item, 'at 41 light-years away, the super Earth TRAPPIST-1 b,' then parallels the semicolon-separated items that follow. ✓
  • Choice C — inverted punctuation: Placing the punctuation in the wrong position breaks the clause-to-list relationship. ✗
  • Choice D — comma before the list: Same problem as A — a comma does not introduce a semicolon-separated list and incorrectly attaches the distance phrase. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
